name: brainspace
documentation:
====================
BrainSpace
====================

BrainSpace is a lightweight cross-platform toolbox primarily intended 
for macroscale gradient mapping and analysis of 
neuroimaging and connectome level data. The current version 
of BrainSpace is available in Python and MATLAB, programming 
languages widely used by the neuroimaging and network neuroscience 
communities. The toolbox also contains several maps that allow for 
exploratory analysis of gradient correspondence with other 
brain-derived features, together with tools to generate spatial null models.

For installation instructions, examples and documentation of BrainSpace see
our `documentation <https://brainspace.readthedocs.io>`_.

Happy gradient analysis! 

License
-----------

The BrainSpace source code is available under the BSD (3-Clause) license.

Support
-----------

If you have problems installing the software or questions about usage 
and documentation, or something else related to BrainSpace, 
you can post to the Issues section of our `repository <https://github.com/MICA-MNI/BrainSpace/issues>`_.

Paper
-----------

If you consider using BrainSpace, please cite our manuscript: 
Vos de Wael R, Benkarim O, Paquola C, Lariviere S, Royer J, Tavakol S, Xu T, Hong S, Langs G, Valk S, Misic B, Milham M, Margulies D, Smallwood J, Bernhardt B (2020). BrainSpace: a toolbox for the analysis of macroscale gradients in neuroimaging and connectomics datasets. Commun Biol 3, 103.

Core development team
-----------------------

* Reinder Vos de Wael, MICA Lab - Montreal Neurological Institute
* Oualid Benkarim, MICA Lab - Montreal Neurological Institute
* Boris Bernhardt, MICA Lab - Montreal Neurological Institute

functions.name: brainspace.vtk_interface.pipeline.connect
documentation:
Connection of two filters.

Connects the output port `port0` of filter `ftr0` with the input port
`port1` of filter `ftr1`.

Parameters
----------
ftr0 : vtkAlgorithm, vtkDataSet, BSAlgorithm or BSDataSet
    The input filter. May be a filter or dataset.
ftr1 : vtkAlgorithm or BSAlgorithm
    The output filter.
port0 : int, optional
    Output port of `ftr0`. Not used if `ftr0` is a dataset. Default is 0.
port1 : int, optional
    Input port of `ftr1`. Default is 0.
add_conn : bool or int, optional
    Connect to specific connection of `port1`.  If False, use
    `SetInputConnection` or `SetInputData` (all other added connections to
    `port1` are removed). Otherwise, use `AddInputConnection` or
    `AddInputData`. If int, add to given connection (e.g.,
    `SetInputConnectionByNumber` or `SetInputDataByNumber`). Only used if
    `port1` accepts more than one connection (i.e., repeatable).
    Default is False.

Returns
-------
ftr1 : BSAlgorithm
    Returns (wrapped) `frt1` after connecting it with the input filter.
Outputs are referred to by port number while
inputs are referred to by both their port number and connection number
(because a single input port can have more than one connection)

functions.name: brainspace.vtk_interface.pipeline.to_data
documentation:
Extract data from filter.

# Parameters

ftr

: Input filter.

port

: Port to get data from. When port is -1, refers to all ports.
  Default is 0.

# Returns

data

: Returns the output of the filter. If port is -1 and number of output
  ports > 1, then return list of outputs.

# Notes

Filters are automatically updated to get the output.

functions.name: brainspace.vtk_interface.pipeline.get_output
documentation:
Get output from filter.

# Parameters

ftr

: Input filter.

as_data

: Return data as BSDataObject instead of {class}`.BSAlgorithm`. If True,
  the filter is automatically updated. Default is True.

update

: Update filter. Only used when `as_data` is False. Default is True.

port

: Output port to update or get data from. Only used when input is
  vtkAlgorithm. When port is -1, refers to all ports. When None, call
  Update() with no arguments. Not used, when `ftr` is a sink
  (i.e., 0 output ports), call Update(). Default is 0.

# Returns

poly

: Returns filter or its output. If port is -1, returns all outputs in a
  list if `as_data == True`.

functions.name: brainspace.vtk_interface.pipeline.serial_connect
documentation:
Connect filters serially.

# Parameters

filters

: Input filters to serially connect. Each input takes one of the
  following formats:

  1. First filter in sequence: `(f0, op=0)`

     > - `f0` (vtkAlgorithm, {class}`.BSAlgorithm`, vtkDataObject or
     >   {class}`.BSDataObject`) - This is the first filter.
     > - `op` (int, optional) - This is the output port of `f0`.
     >   Default is 0.

  2. Last filter in sequence: `(ic=None, ip=0, fn)`

     > - `ic` (int, optional) - This is the input connection of the
     >   input port `ip` of filter `fn`. Default is None.
     > - `ip` (int, optional) - This is the input port of `fn`. Must be
     >   specified when `ic` is not None. Default is 0.
     > - `fn` (vtkAlgorithm or {class}`.BSAlgorithm`) - This is the last
     >   filter.

  3. Intermediate filters: `(ic=None, ip=0, fi, op=0)`

     > - `ic` (int, optional) - This is the input connection of the
     >   input port `ip` of filter `fi`. Default is None.
     > - `ip` (int, optional) - This is the input port of `fi`. Must be
     >   specified when `ic` is not None. Default is 0.
     > - `fi` (vtkAlgorithm or {class}`.BSAlgorithm`) - This is a filter.
     > - `op` (int, optional) - This is the output port of `fi`.
     >   Default is 0.

as_data

: Return data instead of filter. If True, last filter is automatically
  updated. Default is True.

update

: Update last filter. Only used when `as_data == False`.
  Default is True.

port

: Port to update or get data from. When port is -1, refers to all ports.
  Default is 0.

# Returns

output

: Last filter or its output.

# Examples

In VTK:

```pycon
>>> # point source
>>> ps = vtk.vtkPointSource()
>>> ps.SetNumberOfPoints(100)
>>> # delauny
>>> dn = vtk.vtkDelaunay2D()
>>> dn.SetTolerance(0.01)
>>> dn.SetInputConnection(0, ps.GetOutputPort(0))
>>> # smooth
>>> sf = vtk.vtkWindowedSincPolyDataFilter()
>>> sf.SetInputConnection(0, dn.GetOutputPort(0))
>>> sf.SetNumberOfIterations(20)
>>> # update and get output
>>> sf.Update()
>>> sf.GetOutput(0)
(vtkCommonDataModelPython.vtkPolyData)0x7f0134fffb28
```

With `serial_connect` function:

```pycon
>>> from brainspace.vtk_interface.pipeline import serial_connect
>>> # point source
>>> ps = vtk.vtkPointSource()
>>> ps.SetNumberOfPoints(100)
>>> # delauny
>>> dn = vtk.vtkDelaunay2D()
>>> dn.SetTolerance(0.01)
>>> # smooth
>>> sf = vtk.vtkWindowedSincPolyDataFilter()
>>> sf.SetNumberOfIterations(20)
>>> # Connection
>>> serial_connect((ps, 0), (None, 0, dn, 0), (None, 0, sf), as_data=True,
...                port=0)
<brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.BSPolyData at 0x7f0134efb048>
>>> # This can be shortened, since no input connection is needed
>>> serial_connect((ps, 0), (0, dn, 0), (0, sf), as_data=True, port=0)
<brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.BSPolyData at 0x7f0134ee9128>
>>> # And shortened even further since the default input and output
>>> # ports are 0
>>> serial_connect((ps,), (dn,), (sf,), as_data=True, port=0)
<brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.BSPolyData at 0x7f0134ee92b0>
>>> # This is the same
>>> serial_connect(ps, dn, sf)
<brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.BSPolyData at 0x7f0134eee898>
```

functions.name: brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.utils.get_vtk_methods
documentation:
Retrieve Set and Get methods from vtk class or instance.

# Parameters

obj

: VTK class or object.

# Returns

methods

: Dictionary with set and get methods.

# Notes

State methods (see vtkMethodParser) can also be used with options.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import vtk
>>> from brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.base import get_vtk_methods
>>> vtk_map = get_vtk_methods(vtk.vtkPolyDataMapper)
>>> vtk_map.keys()
dict_keys(['set', 'get'])
```

Check setter (state) methods for color mode:

```pycon
>>> vtk_map['set']['colormode']
{'name': 'SetColorMode',
 'options': {
    'default': 'SetColorModeToDefault',
    'directscalars': 'SetColorModeToDirectScalars',
    'mapscalars': 'SetColorModeToMapScalars'}}
```

Check getter methods for array name:

```pycon
>>> vtk_map['get']['arrayname']
'GetArrayName'
```

functions.name: brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.utils.call_vtk
documentation:
Invoke a method on a vtk object.

# Parameters

obj

: VTK object.

method

: Method name.

args

: Arguments to be passed to the method.
  If None, the method is called with no arguments.

# Returns

result

: Return the results of invoking `method` with `args` on `obj`.

# Notes

Use a tuple to pass a None to the method: (None,).

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import vtk
>>> from brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.base import call_vtk
>>> m = vtk.vtkPolyDataMapper()
```

Get array id of the mapper:

```pycon
>>> call_vtk(m, 'GetArrayId', args=None)
-1
>>> m.GetArrayId()
-1
```

Set array id of the mapper to 2:

```pycon
>>> call_vtk(m, 'SetArrayId', args=(2,)) # same as m.SetArrayId(2)
>>> m.GetArrayId()
2
```

documentation:
Get property.

Wraps the `GetProperty` method of `vtkActor` to return a wrapped
property.

Returns
-------
prop : BSProperty
    Actor's property.

Wrapper for vtkActor.

Unresolved requests are forwarded to its property.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers import BSActor
>>> a = BSActor()
>>> a.GetProperty().GetOpacity()
1.0
>>> a.GetOpacity() # It is forwarded to the property
1.0
>>> a.opacity = .5
>>> a.VTKObject.GetProperty().GetOpacity()
0.5
```

documentation:
Append array to attributes.

Parameters
----------
array : 1D or 2D ndarray
    Array to append to the dataset.
name : str or None, optional
    Array name. If None, a random string is generated and returned.
    Default is None.
at : {'point', 'cell', 'field', 'p', 'c', 'f'} or None, optional.
    Attribute to append data to. Points (i.e., 'point' or 'p'),
    cells (i.e., 'cell' or 'c') or field (i.e., 'field' or 'f') data.
    If None, it will attempt to append data to the attributes with
    the same number of elements. Only considers points and cells.
    If both have the same number of elements or the size of the array
    does not coincide with any of them, it raises an exception.
    Default is None.
convert_bool : bool or {'warn', 'raise'}, optional
    If True append array after conversion to uint8. If False,
    array is not appended. If 'warn', issue a warning but append
    the array. If raise, raise an exception.
overwrite : bool or {'warn', 'raise'}, optional
    If True append array even if its name already exists. If False,
    array is not appended, issue a warning. If 'warn', issue a warning
    but append the array. If raise, raise an exception.

Returns
-------
name : str
    Array name used to append the array to the dataset.

classes.name: brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.base.BSVTKObjectWrapperMeta
documentation:
Metaclass for our VTK wrapper

BSVTKObjectWrapper __setattr__ does not allow creating attributes
This metaclass, hides __setattr__ (delegates to object.__setattr__)
during __init__

Postpones setting VTK kwds after __init__ because some subclasses
may forward them to other vtkobjects within.
See for example BSActor, which forwards to its property (GetProperty()).
But this is not known until the actor is created.
E.g.:    actor = BSActor(visibility=1, opacity=.2)
Here visibility is forwarded to vtkActor. But we cannot forward
opacity because it belongs to the actor's property and this is created
after BSVTKObjectWrapper __init__.

methods.name: brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.base.BSVTKObjectWrapper.__getattr__
documentation:
Forwards unknown attribute requests to vtk object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import vtk
>>> from brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers import BSVTKObjectWrapper
>>> m1 = BSVTKObjectWrapper(vtk.vtkPolyDataMapper())
>>> m1.GetArrayId()  # same as self.VTKObject.GetArrayId()
-1
>>> self.arrayId  # same as self.VTKObject.GetArrayId()
-1
```

methods.name: brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.base.BSVTKObjectWrapper.__setattr__
documentation:
Forwards unknown set requests to vtk object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import vtk
>>> from brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers import BSVTKObjectWrapper
>>> m1 = BSVTKObjectWrapper(vtk.vtkPolyDataMapper())
>>> m1.GetArrayId()
-1
>>> self.arrayId = 3  # same as self.VTKObject.SetArrayId(3)
>>> m1.GetArrayId()

```

methods.name: brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.base.BSVTKObjectWrapper.setVTK
documentation:
Invoke set methods on the vtk object.

# Parameters

args

: Setter methods that require no arguments.

kwargs

: key-word arguments can be use for methods that require arguments.
  When several arguments are required, use a tuple.
  Methods that require no arguments can also be used here using
  None as the argument.

# Returns

self

: Return self.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import vtk
>>> from brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers import BSVTKObjectWrapper
>>> m1 = BSVTKObjectWrapper(vtk.vtkPolyDataMapper())
>>> m1.setVTK(arrayId=3, colorMode='mapScalars')
<brainspace.vtk_interface.base.BSVTKObjectWrapper at 0x7f38a4ace320>
>>> m1.arrayId
3
>>> m1.colorModeAsString
'MapScalars'
```

methods.name: brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.base.BSVTKObjectWrapper.getVTK
documentation:
Invoke get methods on the vtk object.

# Parameters

args

: Method that require no arguments.

kwargs

: key-word arguments can be use for methods that require arguments.
  When several arguments are required, use a tuple.
  Methods that require no arguments can also be used here using
  None as the argument.

# Returns

results

: Dictionary of results where the keys are the method names and
  the values the results.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import vtk
>>> from brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers import BSVTKObjectWrapper
>>> m1 = BSVTKObjectWrapper(vtk.vtkPolyDataMapper())
>>> m1.getVTK('arrayId', colorModeAsString=None)
{'arrayId': -1, 'colorModeAsString': 'Default'}
>>> m1.getVTK('colorModeAsString', arrayId=None)
{'colorModeAsString': 'Default', 'arrayId': -1}
>>> m1.getVTK(numberOfInputConnections=0)
{'numberOfInputConnections': 0}
```

methods.name: brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers.base.BSVTKObjectWrapper.vtk_map
documentation: dict: Dictionary of vtk setter and getter methods.

Base class for all classes that wrap VTK objects.

Adapted from dataset_adapter, with additional setVTK and getVTK methods.
Create an instance if class is passed instead of object.

This class holds a reference to the wrapped VTK object. It also
forwards unresolved methods to the underlying object by overloading
\_\_getattr\_\_. This class also supports all VTK setters and getters to be
used like properties/attributes dropping the get/set prefix. This is case
insensitive.

# Parameters

vtkobject

: VTK class or object.

kwargs

: Parameters used to invoke set methods on the vtk object.

# Attributes

VTKObject

: A VTK object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from vtkmodules.vtkRenderingCorePython import vtkPolyDataMapper
>>> from brainspace.vtk_interface.wrappers import BSVTKObjectWrapper
>>> m1 = BSVTKObjectWrapper(vtkPolyDataMapper())
>>> m1
<brainspace.vtk_interface.base.BSVTKObjectWrapper at 0x7f38a4b70198>
>>> m1.VTKObject
(vtkRenderingOpenGL2Python.vtkOpenGLPolyDataMapper)0x7f38a4bee888
```

Passing class and additional keyword arguments:

```pycon
>>> m2 = BSVTKObjectWrapper(vtkPolyDataMapper, arrayId=3,
...                         colorMode='mapScalars')
>>> # Get color name, these are all the same
>>> m2.VTKObject.GetColorModeAsString()
'MapScalars'
>>> m2.GetColorModeAsString()
'MapScalars'
>>> m2.colorModeAsString
'MapScalars'
>>> # Get array id
>>> m2.VTKObject.GetArrayId()
3
>>> m2.GetArrayId()
3
>>> m2.arrayId
3
```

We can change array id and color mode as follows:

```pycon
>>> m2.arrayId = 0
>>> m2.VTKObject.GetArrayId()
0
>>> m2.colorMode = 'default'
>>> m2.VTKObject.GetColorModeAsString()
'Default'
```

functions.name: brainspace.vtk_interface.decorators.append_vtk
documentation:
Decorator to append data to surface.

# Parameters

to: {'point', 'cell', 'field'}, optional

: Append data to PointData, CellData or FieldData. Default is 'point'.

# Returns

wrapped_func: callable

: Wrapped function.

# Notes

All functions using this decorator must:

- Return a ndarray. The size of the array must be consistent with
  the data it will be appended to (e.g., number of points if
  `to == 'point'`), except for FieldData.

- Have the following 2 key-value arguments:

  1. append (bool, optional)
     : If True, append data to surface. Otherwise, return data.
  2. key (str, optional)
     : Array names of data.

modules.name: brainspace.mesh.mesh_cluster
documentation: Clustering and sampling of surface mesh points.

functions.name: brainspace.mesh.mesh_cluster.cluster_points
documentation:
Clustering of surface points.

# Parameters

surf

: Input surface.

n_clusters

: Number of clusters. Default is 100.

is_size

: If True, interpret `n_clusters` as cluster size. Default is False.

mask

: Mask for surface points. Points outside the mask (i.e., False) are
  discarded from clustering. Default is None.

with_centers

: If True, an array of labels with the closest points to the centroid of
  each cluster is returned. Default is True.

random_state

: Random state. Default is None.

approach

: Clustering method: k-means or hierarchical with ward linkage.
  Hierarchical clustering is faster but k-means provides better results.
  Default is 'kmeans'.

n_init

: Number of k-means repetitions. Only used when `approach == 'kmeans'`.
  Default is 3.

n_jobs

: The number of parallel jobs. Only used when `approach == 'kmeans'`.
  Default is 1.

# Returns

cluster_labels

: Array of cluster labels. If `mask` is provided, points out of the mask
  are assigned label 0.

center_labels

: Array with centers labeled with their corresponding cluster label.
  The rest of points is assigned label 0. Returned only if
  `with_centers=True`.

# Notes

Valid cluster labels start from 1. If the mask is provided, zeros are
assigned to the points outside the mask.

functions.name: brainspace.mesh.mesh_cluster.sample_points_clustering
documentation:
Sample equidistant points from surface based on clustering.

# Parameters

surf

: Input surface.

keep

: If float, percentage of points to sample. Must be `0 < keep < 1`.
  If int, number of points to sample. Default is 0.1.

mask

: Mask for surface points. Points outside the mask (i.e., False) are
  discarded from sampling. Default is None.

random_state

: Random state. Default is None.

approach

: Clustering approach: k-means or hierarchical with ward linkage.
  Hierarchical is faster but k-means provides better results.
  Default is 'kmeans'.

n_init

: Number of k-means repetitions. Only used when `approach == 'kmeans'`.
  Default is 3.

n_jobs

: The number of parallel jobs. Only used when `approach == 'kmeans'`.
  Default is 1.

# Returns

sampled

: Array with sampled points marked with 1 in their corresponding
  positions. The rest is 0.

# See Also

{func}`cluster_points`
{func}`sample_points_decimation`

# Notes

This method first clusters the surface points and then selects the points
closest to the centroids as the sampled points.

functions.name: brainspace.mesh.mesh_cluster.sample_points_decimation
documentation:
Sample points from surface based on surface decimation.

# Parameters

surf

: Input surface.

keep

: If float, percentage of points to sample. Must be `0 < keep < 1`.
  If int, number of points to sample. Default is 0.1.

mask

: Mask for surface points. Points outside the mask (i.e., False) are
  discarded from sampling. Default is None.

n_jobs

: Number of jobs. Default is 1.

# Returns

sampled

: Array with sampled points marked with 1 in their corresponding
  positions. The rest is 0.

# Notes

In this method, sampling is based on mesh decimation. Number of sampled
points will most probably not coincide with the requested number.

# See Also

{func}`sample_points_clustering`

functions.name: brainspace.mesh.mesh_correspondence.find_point_correspondence

For each point in the input surface find its corresponding point
in the reference surface.

# Parameters

  Only used when writer accepts format. Default is None.

functions.name: brainspace.mesh.mesh_operations.get_connected_components
documentation:
Get connected components.

Connected components are based on connectivity (and same label if
`labeling` is provided).

# Parameters

surf

: Input surface.

labeling

: Array with labels. If str, it must be in the point data
  attributes of `surf`. Default is None. If provided, connectivity is
  based on neighboring points with the same label.

mask

: Boolean mask. If str, it must be in the point data
  attributes of `surf`. Default is None. If specified, only consider
  points within the mask.

fill

: Value used for entries out of the mask. Only used if the
  `target_mask` is provided. Default is 0.

append

: If True, append array to point data attributes of input surface and
  return surface. Otherwise, only return array. Default is False.

key

: Array name to append to surface's point data attributes. Only used if
  `append == True`. Default is 'components'.

# Returns

output

: 1D array with different labels for each connected component.
  Return ndarray if `append == False`. Otherwise, return input surface
  with the new array.

# Notes

VTK point data does not accept boolean arrays. If the mask is provided as
a string, the mask is built from the corresponding array such that any
value larger than 0 is True.

functions.name: brainspace.mesh.array_operations.propagate_labeling
documentation:
Propagate labeling on surface points.

# Parameters

surf

: Input surface.

labeling

: Array with initial labels. If str, it must be in the point data
  attributes of `surf`. If ndarray, use this array as the initial
  labeling.

no_label

: Value for unlabeled points. Default is np.nan.

mask

: Binary mask. If specified, propagation is only performed on points
  within the mask. Default is None.

alpha

: Clamping factor such that `0 < aplha < 1`. Deault is 0.99.

n_iter

: Maximum number of propagation iterations. Default is 30.

tol

: Convergence tolerance. Default is 0.001.

n_ring

: Consider points in the n-th ring to label the unlabeled points.
  Default is 1.

mode

: Propagation based on connectivity or geodesic distance. Default is
  'connectivity'.

append

: If True, append array to point data attributes of input surface and
  return surface. Otherwise, only return array. Default is False.

key

: Array name to append to surface's point data attributes. Only used if
  `append == True`. Default is 'propagated'.

# Returns

output

: A 1D array with the propagated labeling. Return array if
  `append == False`. Otherwise, return input surface with the
  new array.

# References

- Zhou, D., Bousquet, O., Lal, T. N., Weston, J., & Schölkopf, B. (2004).
  Learning with local and global consistency. Advances in neural
  information processing systems, 16(16), 321-328.

  within the mask. If str, it must be in the point data
  attributes of `surf`. In this case, the mask is composed of all nonzero
  values. Default is None.

kernel

: Smoothing kernel. Default is 'gaussian'.

relax

: Relaxation factor, contribution of neighboring points such that
  `0 < relax < 1`. Default is 0.2.

sigma

: Gaussian kernel width. If None, use standard deviation of egde lengths.
  Default is None.

append

: If True, append array to point data attributes of input surface and
  return surface. Otherwise, only return array. Default is False.

key

: Array name to append to surface's point data attributes. Only used if
  `append == True`. Default is None.

# Returns

output

: A 1D array with the smoothed data. Return array if
  `append == False`. Otherwise, return input surface with the
  new array.

# Raises

ValueError

: If input array is 2D and number of rows does not coincide with number
  of points in `surf`.

# Notes

For 2D arrays, each array is smoothed separately.

Resample point data in source to target surface.

# Parameters

source

is_sphere

  aligned. Default is False.

  `target`. Default is None. If specified, only consider points within
  the mask.

red_func

k

  Only used when `is_sphere==False`. Default is 3.

  `target_mask` is provided. Default is 0.

n_jobs

: Number of parallel jobs. Only used when `is_sphere==False`.
  Default is 1.

append: bool, optional

: If True, append array to point data attributes of target surface and
  return surface. Otherwise, only return resampled arrays.
  Default is False.

key

: Array names to append to target's point data attributes. Only used if
  `append == True`. If None, use names in `source_name`.
  Default is None.

output

  `append == False`. Otherwise, return target surface with the
  new arrays.

This function is meant for the same source and target surfaces but with
different number of points. For other types of resampling, see

modules.name: brainspace.examples.plot_tutorial0
documentation:
# Tutorial 0: Preparing your data for gradient analysis

In this example, we will introduce how to preprocess raw MRI data and how
to prepare it for subsequent gradient analysis in the next tutorials.

## Requirements

For this tutorial, you will need to install the Python package
[load_confounds](https://github.com/SIMEXP/fmriprep_load_confounds). You can
do it using `pip`:

```
pip install load_confounds
```

## Preprocessing

Begin with an MRI dataset that is organized in [BIDS](https://bids.neuroimaging.io/) format. We recommend preprocessing your data
using [fmriprep](http://fmriprep.readthedocs.io/), as described below, but
any preprocessing pipeline will work.

Following is example code to run [fmriprep](http://fmriprep.readthedocs.io/)
using docker from the command line:

```
docker run -ti --rm \
  -v <local_BIDS_data_dir>:/data:ro \
  -v <local_output_dir>:/out poldracklab/fmriprep:latest \
  --output-spaces fsaverage5 \
  --fs-license-file license.txt \
  /data /out participant
```

:::{note}
For this tutorial, it is crucial to output the data onto a cortical surface
template space.
:::

modules.name: brainspace.examples.plot_tutorial3
documentation:
# Tutorial 3: Null models for gradient significance

In this tutorial we assess the significance of correlations between the first
canonical gradient and data from other modalities (curvature, cortical
thickness and T1w/T2w image intensity). A normal test of the significance of
the correlation cannot be used, because the spatial auto-correlation in MRI
data may bias the test statistic. In this tutorial we will show three
approaches for null hypothesis testing: spin permutations, Moran spectral
randomization, and autocorrelation-preserving surrogates based on variogram
matching.

:::{note}
When using either approach to compare gradients to non-gradient markers,
we recommend randomizing the non-gradient markers as these randomizations
need not maintain the statistical independence between gradients.
:::

modules.name: brainspace.examples.plot_tutorial2
documentation:
Tutorial 2: Customizing and aligning gradients
=================================================
In this tutorial you’ll learn about the methods available within the
GradientMaps class. The flexible usage of this class allows for the
customization of gradient computation with different kernels and dimensionality
reductions, as well as aligning gradients from different datasets. This
tutorial will only show you how to apply these techniques.

functions.name: brainspace.null_models.moran.compute_mem
documentation:
Compute Moran eigenvectors map.

# Parameters

w

: Spatial weight matrix or surface. If surface, the weight matrix is
  built based on the inverse geodesic distance between each vertex
  and the vertices in its `n_ring`.

n_ring

: Neighborhood size to build the weight matrix. Only used if user
  provides a surface mesh. Default is 1.

spectrum

: Eigenvalues/vectors to select. If 'all', recover all eigenvectors
  except the smallest one. Otherwise, select all except non-zero
  eigenvectors. Default is 'nonzero'.

tol

: Minimum value for an eigenvalue to be considered non-zero.
  Default is 1e-10.

# Returns

w

: Eigenvalues in descending order. With `n_components = n_vertices - 1`
  if `spectrum == 'all'` and `n_components = n_vertices - n_zero` if
  `spectrum == 'nonzero'`, and `n_zero` is number of zero eigenvalues.

mem

: Eigenvectors of the weight matrix in same order.

# See Also

{func}`.moran_randomization`
{class}`.MoranRandomization`

# References

- Wagner H.H. and Dray S. (2015). Generating spatially constrained
  null models for irregularly spaced data using Moran spectral
  randomization methods. Methods in Ecology and Evolution, 6(10):1169-78.

# See Also

{class}`.MoranRandomization`

  null models for irregularly spaced data using Moran spectral

functions.name: brainspace.null_models.spin._generate_spins
documentation:
Generate rotational spins based on points that lie on a sphere.

# Parameters

points_lh

: Array of points in a sphere, where `n_lh` is the number of points.

points_rh

: Array of points in a sphere, where `n_rh` is the number of points. If
  provided, rotations are derived from the rotations computed for
  `points_lh` by reflecting the rotation matrix across the Y-Z plane.
  Default is None.

unique

: Whether to enforce a one-to-one correspondence between original points
  and rotated ones. If true, the Hungarian algorithm is used.
  Default is False.

n_rep

: Number of random rotations. Default is 100.

surface_algorithm

: For 'CIVET', no flip is required to generate the spins for the right
  hemisphere. Only used when `points_rh is not None`.
  Default is 'FreeSurfer'.

random_state

: Random state. Default is None.

# Returns

result

: Spin indices for left points (and also right, if provided).

# References

- Alexander-Bloch A, Shou H, Liu S, Satterthwaite TD, Glahn DC,
  Shinohara RT, Vandekar SN and Raznahan A (2018). On testing for spatial
  correspondence between maps of human brain structure and function.
  NeuroImage, 178:540-51.
- Blaser R and Fryzlewicz P (2016). Random Rotation Ensembles.
  Journal of Machine Learning Research, 17(4): 1–26.
- <https://netneurotools.readthedocs.io>

Generate null data using spin permutations.

spheres

: Dictionary of points in a sphere, for left ('lh' key) and
  right ('rh' key) hemispheres. The right hemisphere is optional. If
  provided, rotations are derived from the rotations computed for
  `points_lh` by reflecting the rotation matrix across the Y-Z plane.

data

: Dictionary of data to randomize. Array of variables arranged in
  columns for each hemisphere.

unique

: Whether to enforce a one-to-one correspondence between original points
  and rotated ones. If true, the Hungarian algorithm is used.
  Default is False.

n_rep

: Number of random rotations. Default is 100.

surface_algorithm

: For 'CIVET', no flip is required to generate the spins for the right
  hemisphere. Only used when `points_rh is not None`.
  Default is 'FreeSurfer'.

random_state

: Random state. Default is None.

# Returns

rand_lh

: Permutations of data in left hemisphere.

rand_rh

: Permutations of data in right hemisphere. Only if right data and
  sphere are provided.

# See Also

{class}`.SpinPermutations`

# References

- Alexander-Bloch A, Shou H, Liu S, Satterthwaite TD, Glahn DC,

  correspondence between maps of human brain structure and function.

documentation:
Export distance matrix to memory-mapped array.

Parameters
----------
dist_file : filename
    Path to `delimiter`-separated distance matrix file
output_dir : filename
    Path to directory in which output files will be written
maskfile : filename or ndarray or None, default None
    Path to a neuroimaging/txt file containing a mask, or a mask
    represented as a numpy array. Mask scalars are cast to boolean, and
    all elements not equal to zero will be masked.
delimiter : str
    Delimiting character in `dist_file`

Returns
-------
dict
    Keys are 'D' and 'index'; values are absolute paths to the
    corresponding binary files on disk.

Notes
-----
Each row of the distance matrix is sorted before writing to file. Thus, a
second mem-mapped array is necessary, the i-th row of which contains
argsort(d[i]).
If `maskfile` is not None, a binary mask.txt file will also be written to
the output directory.

documentation: 1D ndarray : distances at which variogram is evaluated

Spatial autocorrelation-preserving surrogate brain maps wih sampling.

# Parameters

ns

: Take a subsample of `ns` rows from `D` when fitting variograms.
  Default is 500.

deltas

: Proportion of neighbors to include for smoothing, in (0, 1\]
  Default is [0.1,0.2,...,0.9].

kernel

: Kernel with which to smooth permuted maps:

  : 'gaussian' : Gaussian function.
    'exp' : Exponential decay function.
    'invdist' : Inverse distance.
    'uniform' : Uniform weights (distance independent).

  Default is 'exp'.

pv

: Percentile of the pairwise distance distribution at which to
  truncate during variogram fitting. Default is 25.

nh

: Number of uniformly spaced distances at which to compute variogram.
  Default is 25.

knn

: Number of nearest regions to keep in the neighborhood of each region.
  Default is 1000.

b

: Gaussian kernel bandwidth for variogram smoothing. if None,
  three times the distance interval spacing is used.

resample

: Resample surrogate maps' values from target brain map.
  Default is False.

n_rep

: Number of randomizations (i.e., surrogate maps). Default is 100.

random_state

: Random state. Default is None.

verbose

: Print surrogate count each time new surrogate map created

# See Also

{class}`.SurrogateMaps`
{class}`.SpinPermutations`
{class}`.MoranRandomization`

# Notes

Passing resample=True will preserve the distribution of values in the
target map, at the expense of worsening simulated surrogate maps'
variograms fits. This worsening will increase as the empirical map
more strongly deviates from normality.

functions.name: brainspace.gradient.embedding.diffusion_mapping
documentation:
Compute diffusion map of affinity matrix.

# Parameters

adj

: Affinity matrix.

n_components

: Number of eigenvectors. If None, selection of `n_components` is based
  on 95% drop-off in eigenvalues. When `n_components` is None,
  the maximum number of eigenvectors is restricted to
  `n_components <= sqrt(n)`. Default is 10.

alpha

: Anisotropic diffusion parameter, `0 <= alpha <= 1`. Default is 0.5.

diffusion_time

: Diffusion time or scale. If `diffusion_time == 0` use multi-scale
  diffusion maps. Default is 0.

random_state

: Random state. Default is None.

# Returns

v

: Eigenvectors of the affinity matrix in same order.

w

: Eigenvalues of the affinity matrix in descending order.

# References

- Coifman, R.R.; S. Lafon. (2006). "Diffusion maps". Applied and
  Computational Harmonic Analysis 21: 5-30. doi:10.1016/j.acha.2006.04.006
- Joseph W.R., Peter E.F., Ann B.L., Chad M.S. Accurate parameter
  estimation for star formation history in galaxies using SDSS spectra.

methods.name: brainspace.gradient.gradient.GradientMaps.fit
documentation:
Compute gradients and alignment.

# Parameters

x

: Input matrix or list of matrices.

gamma

: Inverse kernel width. Only used if `kernel == 'gaussian'`.
  If None, `gamma=1/n_feat`. Default is None.

sparsity

: Proportion of the smallest elements to zero-out for each row.
  Default is 0.9.

n_iter

: Number of iterations for procrustes alignment. Default is 10.

reference

: Initial reference for procrustes alignments. Only used when
  `alignment == 'procrustes'`. Default is None.

kwargs

: Additional keyword parameters passed to the embedding approach.

# Returns

self

: Returns self.

Gradient maps.

# Parameters

n_components

: Number of gradients. Default is 10.

approach

: Embedding approach. Default is 'dm'. It can be a string or instance:

  - 'dm' or {class}`.DiffusionMaps`: embedding using diffusion maps.
  - 'le' or {class}`.LaplacianEigenmaps`: embedding using Laplacian
    eigenmaps.
  - 'pca' or {class}`.PCAMaps`: embedding using PCA.

kernel

: Kernel function to build the affinity matrix. Possible options:
  {'pearson', 'spearman', 'cosine', 'normalized_angle', 'gaussian'}.
  If callable, must receive a 2D array and return a 2D square array.
  If None, use input matrix. Default is 'normalized_angle'.

alignment

: Alignment approach. Only used when two or more datasets are provided.
  If None, no alignment is performed. If `object`, it accepts an instance
  of {class}`.ProcrustesAlignment`. Default is None.

  - If 'procrustes', datasets are aligned using generalized procrustes
    analysis.
  - If 'joint', datasets are embedded simultaneously based on a joint
    affinity matrix built from the individual datasets. This option is
    only available for 'dm' and 'le' approaches.

random_state

: Random state. Default is None.

# Attributes

[lambdas]

: Eigenvalues for each datatset.

[gradients]

: Gradients (i.e., eigenvectors).

[aligned]

: Aligned gradients. None if `alignment is None` or only one dataset
  is used.

functions.name: brainspace.gradient.kernels.compute_affinity
documentation:
Compute affinity matrix.

# Parameters

x

: Input matrix.

kernel

: Kernel function. If None, only sparsify. Default is None.
  Valid options:

  - If 'pearson', use Pearson's correlation coefficient.
  - If 'spearman', use Spearman's rank correlation coefficient.
  - If 'cosine', use cosine similarity.
  - If 'normalized_angle': use normalized angle between two vectors. This
    option is based on cosine similarity but provides similarities
    bounded between 0 and 1.
  - If 'gaussian', use Gaussian kernel or RBF.

sparsity

: Proportion of smallest elements to zero-out for each row.
  If None, do not sparsify. Default is 0.9.

pre_sparsify

: Sparsify prior to building the affinity. If False, sparsify the final
  affinity matrix.

non_negative

: If True, zero-out negative values. Otherwise, do nothing.

gamma

: Inverse kernel width. Only used if `kernel == 'gaussian'`.
  If None, `gamma = 1./n_feat`. Default is None.

# Returns

affinity

: Affinity matrix.

functions.name: brainspace.plotting.surface_plotting.build_plotter
documentation:
Build plotter arranged according to the `layout`.

# Parameters

surfs

: Dictionary of surfaces.

layout

: Array of surface keys in `surfs`. Specifies how window is arranged.

array_name

: Names of point data array to plot for each layout entry.
  Use a tuple with multiple array names to plot multiple arrays
  (overlays) per layout entry. If None, plot surfaces without any array
  data. Default is None.

view

: View for each layout entry. Possible views are {'lateral',
  'medial', 'ventral', 'dorsal'}. If None, use default view.
  Default is None.

color_bar

: Location where color bars are rendered. If None, color bars are not
  included. Default is None.

color_range

: Range for each array name. If 'sym', uses a symmetric range. Only used
  if array has positive and negative values. Default is None.

share

: If `share == 'row'`, point data for surfaces in the same row share
  same data range. If `share == 'col'`, the same but for columns.
  If `share == 'both'`, all data shares same range. If True, similar
  to `share == 'both'`. Default is False.

label_text

: Label text for column/row. Possible keys are {'left', 'right',
  'top', 'bottom'}, which indicate the location. Default is None.

cmap

: Color map name (from matplotlib) for each array name.
  Default is 'viridis'.

nan_color

: Color for nan values. Default is (0, 0, 0, 1).

zoom

: Zoom applied to the surfaces in each layout entry.

background

: Background color. Default is (1, 1, 1).

size

: Window size. Default is (400, 400).

kwargs

: Additional arguments passed to the renderers, actors, mapper, color_bar
  or plotter. Keywords starting with:

  - '' are passed to the renderers.
  - '' are passed to the actors.
  - '' are passed to the mappers.
  - '' are passed to color bar actors.
  - '' are passed to color text actors.

  The rest of keywords are passed to the plotter.

# Returns

plotter

: An instance of Plotter.

# See Also

{func}`plot_surf`
{func}`plot_hemispheres`

# Notes

If sequences, shapes of `array_name`, `view` and `zoom` must be equal
or broadcastable to the shape of `layout`. Renderer keywords must also
be broadcastable to the shape of `layout`.

If sequences, shapes of `cmap` and `cbar_range` must be equal or
broadcastable to the shape of `array_name`, including the number of array
names per entry. Actor and mapper keywords must also be broadcastable to
the shape of `array_name`.

functions.name: brainspace.plotting.surface_plotting.plot_surf
documentation:
Plot surfaces arranged according to the `layout`.

# Parameters

surfs

: Dictionary of surfaces.

layout

: Array of surface keys in `surfs`. Specifies how window is arranged.

array_name

: Names of point data array to plot for each layout entry.
  Use a tuple with multiple array names to plot multiple arrays
  (overlays) per layout entry. If None, plot surfaces without any array
  data. Default is None.

view

: View for each layout entry. Possible views are {'lateral',
  'medial', 'ventral', 'dorsal'}. If None, use default view.
  Default is None.

color_bar

: Location where color bars are rendered. If None, color bars are not
  included. Default is None.

color_range

: Range for each array name. If 'sym', uses a symmetric range. Only used
  if array has positive and negative values. Default is None.

share

: If `share == 'row'`, point data for surfaces in the same row share
  same data range. If `share == 'col'`, the same but for columns.
  If `share == 'both'`, all data shares same range. If True, similar
  to `share == 'both'`. Default is False.

label_text

: Label text for column/row. Possible keys are {'left', 'right',
  'top', 'bottom'}, which indicate the location. Default is None.

cmap

: Color map name (from matplotlib) for each array name.
  Default is 'viridis'.

nan_color

: Color for nan values. Default is (0, 0, 0, 1).

zoom

: Zoom applied to the surfaces in each layout entry.

background

: Background color. Default is (1, 1, 1).

size

: Window size. Default is (400, 400).

interactive

: Whether to enable interaction. Default is True.

embed_nb

: Whether to embed figure in notebook. Only used if running in a
  notebook. Default is False.

screenshot

: Take a screenshot instead of rendering. Default is False.

filename

: Filename to save the screenshot. Default is None.

transparent_bg

: Whether to us a transparent background. Only used if
  `screenshot==True`. Default is False.

scale

: Scale (magnification). Only used if `screenshot==True`.
  Default is None.

return_plotter: bool, optional

: If True, return plotter instead of returning image or rendering.

suppress_warnings

: Whether to suppress warnings. Default is False.

kwargs

: Additional arguments passed to the renderers, actors, mapper or
  plotter. Keywords starting with:

  - '' are passed to the renderers.
  - '' are passed to the actors.
  - '' are passed to the mappers.

  The rest of keywords are passed to the plotter.

# Returns

figure

: Figure to plot. None if using vtk for rendering (i.e.,
  `embed_nb == False`).

# See Also

{func}`build_plotter`
{func}`plot_hemispheres`

# Notes

If sequences, shapes of `array_name`, `view` and `zoom` must be equal
or broadcastable to the shape of `layout`. Renderer keywords must also
be broadcastable to the shape of `layout`.

If sequences, shapes of `cmap` and `cbar_range` must be equal or
broadcastable to the shape of `array_name`, including the number of array
names per entry. Actor and mapper keywords must also be broadcastable to
the shape of `array_name`.

functions.name: brainspace.plotting.surface_plotting.plot_hemispheres
documentation:
Plot left and right hemispheres in lateral and medial views.

# Parameters

surf_lh

: Left hemisphere.

surf_rh

: Right hemisphere.

array_name

: Name of point data array to plot. If ndarray, the array is split for
  the left and right hemispheres. If list, plot one row per array.
  If None, plot surfaces without any array data. Default is None.

color_bar

: Plot color bar for each array (row). Default is False.

color_range

: Range for each array name. If 'sym', uses a symmetric range. Only used
  if array has positive and negative values. Default is None.

label_text

: Label text for column/row. Possible keys are {'left', 'right',
  'top', 'bottom'}, which indicate the location. Default is None.

layout_style

: Layout style for hemispheres. If 'row', layout is a single row
  alternating lateral and medial views, from left to right. If 'grid',
  layout is a 2x2 grid, with lateral views in the top row, medial
  views in the bottom row, and left and right columns. Default is 'row'.

nan_color

: Color for nan values. Default is (0, 0, 0, 1).

zoom

: Zoom applied to the surfaces in each layout entry.

background

: Background color. Default is (1, 1, 1).

cmap

: Color map name (from matplotlib). Default is 'viridis'.

size

: Window size. Default is (800, 200).

interactive

: Whether to enable interaction. Default is True.

embed_nb

: Whether to embed figure in notebook. Only used if running in a
  notebook. Default is False.

screenshot

: Take a screenshot instead of rendering. Default is False.

filename

: Filename to save the screenshot. Default is None.

transparent_bg

: Whether to us a transparent background. Only used if
  `screenshot==True`. Default is False.

scale

: Scale (magnification). Only used if `screenshot==True`.
  Default is None.

kwargs

: Additional arguments passed to the plotter.

# Returns

figure

: Figure to plot. None if using vtk for rendering (i.e.,
  `embed_nb == False`).

{func}`plot_surf`

functions.name: brainspace.plotting.base._create_grid
documentation:
Create bounds for vtk rendering

# Parameters

nrow

: Number of rows. If array-like, must be an array with values in
  ascending order between 0 and 1.

ncol

: Number of columns. If array-like, must be an array with values in
  ascending order between 0 and 1.

# Returns

grid: ndarray, shape = (nrow, ncol, 4)

: Grid for vtk rendering.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> _create_grid(1, 2)
array([[[0. , 0. , 0.5, 1. ],
        [0.5, 0. , 1. , 1. ]]])
>>> _create_grid(1, [0, .5, 1])
array([[[0. , 0. , 0.5, 1. ],
        [0.5, 0. , 1. , 1. ]]])
>>> _create_grid(1, [0, .5, .9])
array([[[0. , 0. , 0.5, 1. ],
        [0.5, 0. , 0.9, 1. ]]])
>>> _create_grid(1, [0, .5, .9, 1])
array([[[0. , 0. , 0.5, 1. ],
        [0.5, 0. , 0.9, 1. ],
        [0.9, 0. , 1. , 1. ]]])
>>> _create_grid(2, [.5, .6, .7])
array([[[0.5, 0.5, 0.6, 1. ],
        [0.6, 0.5, 0.7, 1. ]],
```

> \[[0.5, 0. , 0.6, 0.5],
>
> : [0.6, 0. , 0.7, 0.5]\]\])

functions.name: brainspace.utils.parcellation.map_to_mask
documentation:
Assign data to mask.

# Parameters

values

: Source array of values.

mask

: Mask of boolean values. Data is mapped to mask.
  If `values` is 2D, the mask is applied according to `axis`.

fill

: Value used to fill elements outside the mask. Default is 0.

axis

: If `axis == 0` map rows. Otherwise, map columns. Default is 0.

# Returns

output

: Values mapped to mask. If `values` is 1D, shape (n_mask,).
  When `values` is 2D, shape (n_samples, n_mask) if `axis == 0` and
  (n_mask, n_samples) otherwise.

functions.name: brainspace.utils.parcellation.map_to_labels
documentation:
Map data in source to target according to their labels.

Target labels are sorted in ascending order, such that the smallest label
indexes the value at position 0 in `source_val`. If `source_lab` is
specified, any label in `target_lab` must be in `source_lab`.

# Parameters

source_val

: Source array of values.

target_lab

: Target labels.

mask

: If mask is not None, only consider target labels in mask.
  If int, it indicates the label denoting label to discard.
  Default is None.

fill

: Value used to fill elements outside the mask. Only used if mask is not
  None. Default is 0.

axis

: If `axis == 0`, map rows. Otherwise, map columns. Default is 0.

source_lab

: Source labels for source values. If None, use unique labels in
  `target_lab` in ascending order. Default is None.

# Returns

target_val

: Target array with corresponding source values.

functions.name: brainspace.utils.parcellation.reduce_by_labels
documentation:
Summarize data in `values` according to `labels`.

# Parameters

values

: Array of values.

labels

: Labels used summarize values.

weights

: Weights associated with labels. Only used when `red_op` is
  'average', 'mean', 'sum' or 'mode'. Weights are not normalized.
  Default is None.

target_labels

: Target labels. Arrange new array following the ordering of labels
  in the `target_labels`. When None, new array is arranged in ascending
  order of `labels`. Default is None.

red_op

: How to summarize data. If str, options are: {'min', 'max', 'sum',
  'mean', 'median', 'mode', 'average'}. If callable, it should receive
  a 1D array of values, array of weights (or None) and return a scalar
  value. Default is 'mean'.

dtype

: Data type of output array. When None, if `red_op` in
  {'min', 'max', 'sum', 'mode'}, output is same type as `values`,
  otherwise output is float. Default is None.

axis

: If `axis == 0`, apply to each row (reduce number of columns per row).
  Otherwise, apply to each column (reduce number of rows per column).
  Default is 0.

# Returns

target_values

: Summarized target values.
